prompt: California passed a major tobacco control program in 1988. I want to estimate its effect on per-capita cigarette sales using other US states as controls. I have annual cigarette sales data for all 50 states from 1970-2000. California is by far the largest state and had uniquely low smoking rates even before the program. Can you build a synthetic control analysis?
assertions: Identifies this as a Synthetic Control design
Warns about the convex hull problem: California may be an outlier that donors can't approximate
Asks the user about California's pre-treatment characteristics relative to donor states
Uses CausalPy's SyntheticControl class with WeightedSumFitter
Checks pre-treatment fit quality (RMSE or visual comparison of synthetic vs actual California pre-1988)
If pre-treatment fit is poor, flags this as a threat to validity rather than ignoring it
Runs at least one robustness check: leave-one-out donors, placebo treatments on control states, or pre-treatment fit assessment
Reports the effect with uncertainty (credible interval or posterior distribution)
States limitations: if California is outside the convex hull of donors, the synthetic counterfactual may be unreliable
Does not make strong causal claims if pre-treatment fit is poor
